failure_surface: IndexError: tuple index out of range when accessing imported_data[1][col_idx]
root_cause: The agent mixed 0-based Python tuple indexing with 1-based Excel-style column iteration. When using worksheet[row_number], openpyxl returns a tuple indexed from 0, but the loop iterated col_idx from 1 to max_column, causing out-of-bounds access.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Use the .cell(row, column) method consistently with 1-based indexing throughout openpyxl operations, or adjust tuple indexing to 0-based when accessing worksheet[row_number] results.
evidence: Agent used: for col_idx in range(1, imported_data.max_column + 1): headers.append(imported_data[1][col_idx].value)
Error traceback shows IndexError at imported_data[1][col_idx] where col_idx reaches values beyond tuple length
Earlier successful exploration code used cell().value and iter_rows() pattern without errors
Final solution reused incorrect indexing pattern instead of verified working approach

memory_items.title: Consistent Indexing Convention in openpyxl
memory_items.lesson: Always use the .cell(row=row, column=column) method with 1-based indexing for openpyxl cell access, not direct tuple indexing on worksheet[row_number]
memory_items.generalization: Direct tuple indexing on worksheet[row] returns 0-based tuples; prefer .cell() method for consistent 1-based Excel-style coordinate handling

memory_items.title: Verify Iteration Patterns Against Data Source
memory_items.lesson: When iterating over cell ranges, confirm the indexing base of the returned data structure matches your loop variables
memory_items.generalization: Validate array/tuple indexing conventions before writing loops over spreadsheet data structures

memory_items.title: Reuse Working Code Patterns Across Solution Steps
memory_items.lesson: Apply verified exploration patterns consistently in the final solution to avoid re-introducing known bugs
memory_items.generalization: When multiple access patterns work during exploration, select one consistent approach and apply it throughout the implementation

edits.content:
### Indexing Consistency
- **Avoid Mixing Index Bases:** openpyxl's .cell(row, column) method uses 1-based indexing; worksheet[row] returns a 0-indexed tuple. Do not mix these conventions in the same operation.
- **Preferred Access Pattern:** Use `.cell(row=r, column=c).value` consistently across all cell read/write operations to maintain 1-based coordinate clarity.
- **Tuple Caution:** If accessing worksheet[row] directly, remember to adjust loop indices to 0-based or use enumerate() safely.
